2606.14145.title: Personal Care Utility: Health as Everyday Infrastructure
2606.14145.paper_summary: Healthcare is essential, expert, and episodic by design - built around the roughly one hour per year a person spends with a clinician. The 8,759 hours outside clinical settings, where eating, sleeping, movement, medication, and stress actually shape long-term health, have no comparable infrastructure. The bottleneck for personalized health is not raw data or reasoning capability; it is the absence of that infrastructure layer. This paper introduces the Personal Care Utility (PCU): a layered, event-driven architecture proposed as the missing utility for everyday health, in the way that payments, networks, and power are utilities for their domains. PCU organizes continuous personal signals into semantically meaningful life events through a Personicle, estimates dynamic health state against personal baselines, reasons about cause and context, and routes guidance through an orchestrator that separates clinical decision logic, behavioral strategy selection, and natural-language expression. This separation lets large language models support reasoning and communication while keeping safety-critical clinical decisions grounded in validated evidence. We instantiate PCU for Type 2 Diabetes - turning CGM, meal, activity, medication, sleep, stress, and clinical data into glycemic events, individualized state estimates, causal explanations, and knowledge-grounded interventions. A day-in-the-life scenario shows the same infrastructure producing real-time nudges, weekly summaries, medication check-ins, silence, or deterministic safety alerts depending on context and risk. We close with how PCU generalizes to other chronic conditions and the governance questions any always-on personal health utility must address. The result is a blueprint that treats personalization not as a final messaging layer, but as an architectural property of everyday health guidance.
2606.14145.paper_summary_zh: 醫療保健是必不可少的、專業的，並且是按設計進行的——圍繞著一個人每年與臨床醫生相處的大約一小時。 在臨床環境之外的 8,759 小時中，飲食、睡眠、運動、用藥和壓力實際上塑造了長期健康，卻沒有相應的基礎設施。 個性化健康的瓶頸不是原始數據或推理能力，而是缺乏那一層基礎設施。 本文介紹了個人護理效用（PCU）：一種分層的、事件驅動的架構，作為日常健康的缺失效用，正如支付、網絡和電力是其領域的效用。 PCU 通過 Personicle 將持續的個人信號組織成語義上有意義的生活事件，根據個人基準估算動態健康狀態，推理原因和背景，並通過一個協調者路由指導，該協調者將臨床決策邏輯、行為策略選擇和自然語言表達分開。 這種分離使大型語言模型能夠支持推理和交流，同時使安全關鍵的臨床決策基於經過驗證的證據。 我們為 2 型糖尿病實現了 PCU——將 CGM、餐飲、活動、用藥、睡眠、壓力和臨床數據轉化為血糖事件、個性化狀態估算、因果解釋和基於知識的干預。 一個日常生活場景顯示相同的基礎設施根據上下文和風險產生實時提示、每週摘要、用藥檢查、靜默或確定性安全警報。 我們最後討論 PCU 如何推廣到其他慢性病以及任何始終在線的個人健康效用必須解決的治理問題。 最終結果是一個藍圖，將個性化視為日常健康指導的架構特性，而不是最終的消息層。
2606.14145.author: Mahyar Abbasian et.al.
2606.14145.authors: Mahyar Abbasian, Elahe Khatibi, Saba A. Farahani, Nitish Nagesh, Arshia Ilaty, Hooman Sajjadi, Amir Rahmani, Ramesh Jain
